author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XIII. 34, 35.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “All these things spake Jesus unto the multitudes in parables, and without a parable spake He notunto them; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things that have been kept secretfrom the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Mark saith, “As they were able to hear it, He spake the word unto them in parables.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then pointing out that He is not making a new thing, He brings in the Prophet also, proclaiming beforehand this His manner of teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to teach us the purpose of Christ, how He discoursed in this manner, not that they might be ignorant, but that He might lead them to inquiry, he added, “And without a parable spake He nothing unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely He did say many things without a parable; but then nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for all this no man asked Him questions, whereas the Prophets, we know, they were often questioning: as Ezekiel,for instance; as many others: but these did no such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely His sayings were enough to cast them into perplexity, and to stir them up to the inquiry; for indeed a very sore punishment was threatened by those parables: however, not even so were they moved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He left them and went away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then,” saith he, “Jesus sent the multitudes away,and went into His house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not one of the Scribes follows Him; whence it is clear that for no other purpose did they follow, than to take hold of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when they marked not His sayings, thenceforth He let them be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And His disciples come unto Him, asking Him concerning the parable of the tares;”although at times wishing to learn, and afraidto ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then arose their confidence in this instance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They had been told, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of Heaven;” and they were emboldened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also they ask in private; not as grudging the multitude, but observing their Master’s law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “To these,” saith He, “it is not given.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why may it be that they let pass the parable of the leaven, and of the mustard seed, and inquire concerning this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They let those pass, as being plainer; but about this, as having an affinity to that before spoken, and as setting forth something more than it, they are desirous to learn (since He would not have spoken the same to them a second time); for indeed they saw how severe was the threatening therein uttered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither doth He blame them, but rather completes His previous statements.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, as I am always saying, the parables must not be explained throughout word for word, since many absurdities will follow; this even He Himself is teaching us here in thus interpreting this parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He saith not at all who the servants are that came to Him, but, implying that He brought them in, for the sake of some order, and to make up the picture, He omits that part, and interprets those that are most urgent and essential, and for the sake of which the parable was spoken; signifying Himself to be Judge and Lord of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He answered,” so it is said, “and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of Man; the field is the world, the good seed, these are the children of the kingdom, but the tares are the children of the wicked one; the enemy that soweth them is the devil; and the harvest is the end of the world, and the reapers are angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The Son of Man shall send His angels, and they shall gather out of His kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity; and shall cast them into the furnace of fire, there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas He Himself is the sower, and that of His own field, and out of His own kingdom He gathers, it is quite clear that the present world also is His.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark His unspeakable love to man, and His leaning to bounty, and His disinclination to punishment; in that, when He sows, He sows in His own person, but when He punishes, it is by others, that is, by the angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not because it will be just so much only, but because this star is surpassed in brightness by none that we know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He uses the comparisons that are known to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet surely elsewhere He saith, the harvest is already come; as when He saith of the Samaritans, “Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “The harvest truly is plenteous, but the laborers are few.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then saith He there, that the harvest is already come, while here He said, it is yet to be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: According to another signification.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how having elsewhere said, “One soweth, and another reapeth,”doth He here say, it is Himself that soweth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because there again, He was speaking, to distinguish the apostles, not from Himself, but from the prophets, and that in the case of the Jews and Samaritans.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since certainly it was He who sowed through the prophets also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And at times He calls this self-same thing both harvest and sowing, naming it with relation, now to one thing, now to another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus when He is speaking of the conviction and obedience of His converts, He calls the thing “a harvest,” as though He had accomplished all; but when He is seeking after the fruit of their hearing, He calls it seed, and the end, harvest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how saith He elsewhere, that “the righteous are caught up first?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because they are indeed caught up first, but Christ being come, those others are given over to punishment, and then the former depart into the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because they must be in heaven, but He Himself is to come and judge all men here; having passed sentence upon these, like some king He rises with His friends, leading them to that blessed portion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that the punishment is twofold, first to be burnt up, and then to fall from that glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore doth He still go on, when the others have withdrawn, to speak to these also in parables?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They had become wiser by His sayings, so as even to understand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At any rate, to them He saith afterwards,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Have ye understood all these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They say unto Him, Yea, Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So completely, together with its other objects, did the parable effect this too, that it made them more clear sighted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “The Kingdom of Heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field, the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like unto a merchant man seeking goodly pearls, who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Much as in the other place, the mustard seed and the leaven have but some little difference from each other, so here also these two parables, that of the treasure and that of the pearl.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This being of course signified by both, that we ought to value the gospel above all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the former indeed, of the leaven and of the mustard seed, was spoken with a view to the power of the gospel, and to its surely prevailing over the world; but these declare its value, and great price.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as it extends itself like mustard seed, and prevails like leaven, so it is precious like a pearl, and affords full abundance like a treasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We are then to learn not this only, that we ought to strip ourselves of everything else, and cling to the gospel, but also that we are to do so with joy; and when a man is dispossessing himself of his goods, he is to know that the transaction is gain, and not loss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how both the gospel is hid in the world, and the good things in the gospel?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Except thou sell all, thou buyest not; except thou have such a soul, anxious and inquiring, thou findest not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Two things therefore are requisite, abstinence from worldly matters, and watchfulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He saith “One seeking goodly pearls, who when he had found one of great price, sold all and bought it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the truth is one, and not in many divisions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And much as he that hath the pearl knows indeed himself that he is rich, but others know not, many times, that he is holding it in his hand (for there is no corporeal bulk); just so also with the gospel, they that have hold of it know that they are rich, but the unbelievers, not knowing of this treasure, are in ignorance also of our wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, that we may not be confident in the gospel merely preached, nor think that faith only suffices us for salvation, He utters also another, an awful parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That of the net.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the kingdom of Heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind; which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherein doth this differ from the parable of the tares?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there too the one are saved, the other perish; but there, for choosing of wicked doctrines; and those before this again, for not giving heed to His sayings, but these for wickedness of life; who are the most wretched of all, having attained to His knowledge, and being caught, but not even so capable of being saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely He saith elsewhere, that the shepherd Himself separates them, but here He saith the angels do this;and so with respect to the tares.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At one time He discourses to them in a way more suited to their dullness,at another time in a higher strain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this parable He interprets without so much as being asked, but of His own motion He explained it by one part of it, and increased their awe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For lest, on being told, “They cast the bad away,” thou shouldest suppose that ruin to be without danger; by His interpretation He signified the punishment, saying, “They will cast them into the furnace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He declared the gnashing of teeth, and the anguish, that it is unspeakable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how many are the ways of destruction?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By the rock, by the thorns, by the wayside, by the tares, by the net.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not without reason therefore did He say, “Broad is the way that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go awayby it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then uttered all this, and concluded His discourse in a tone to cause fear, and signified that these are the majority of cases (for He dwelt more on them).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Have ye understood all these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They say unto Him, Yea, Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then because they understood, He again praises them, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Therefore every Scribe, which is instructed in the Kingdom of Heaven,is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore elsewhere also He saith, “I will send you wise men and scribes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how so far from excluding the Old Testament, He even commends it, and speaks publicly in favor of it, calling it “a treasure”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that as many as are ignorant of the divine Scriptures cannot be “householders;” such as neither have of themselves, nor receive of others, but neglect their own case, perishing with famine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not these only, but the heretics too,are excluded from this blessing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they bring not forth things new and old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they have not the old things, wherefore neither have they the new; even as they who have not the new, neither have they the old, but are deprived of both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these are bound up and interwoven one with another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then hear, as many of us as neglect the reading of the Scriptures, to what harm we are subjecting ourselves, to what poverty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when are we to apply ourselves to the real practice of virtue, who do not so much as know the very laws according to which our practice should be guided?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But while the rich, those who are mad about wealth, are constantly shaking out their garments, that they may not become moth-eaten; dost thou, seeing forgetfulness worse than any moth wasting thy soul, neglect conversing with books? dost thou not thrust away from thee the pest, adorn thy soul, look continually upon the image of virtue, and acquaint thyself with her members and her head?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For she too hath a head and members more seemly than any graceful and beautiful body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, saith one, is the head of virtue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ also begins with it, saying, “Blessed are the poor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This head hath not locks and ringlets, but beauty, such as to gain God’s favor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Unto whom shall I look,” saith He, “but unto him that is meek and humble, and trembleth at my words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, “Mine eyes are upon the meek of the earth.”And, “The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a contrite heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This head, instead of locks, and flowing hair, bears sacrifices acceptable to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is a golden altar, and a spiritual place of sacrifice;“For a contrite spirit is a sacrifice to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the mother of wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If a man have this, he will have the rest also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou seen a head such as thou hadst never seen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou see the face too, or rather mark it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark then for the present its color, how ruddy, and blooming, and very engaging; and observe what are its ingredients.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Well, and what are they?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shame-facedness and blushing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also some one saith, “Before a shamefaced man shall go favor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This sheds much beauty over the other members also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though thou mix ten thousand colors, thou wilt not produce such a bloom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou wilt see the eyes also, behold them exactly delineated with decency and temperance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore they become also so beautiful and sharpsighted, as to behold even the Lord Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Blessed,” saith He, “are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And her mouth is wisdom and understanding, and the knowledge of spiritual hymns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And her heart, acquaintance with Scripture, and maintenance of sound doctrines, and benevolence, and kindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as without this last there is no living, so without that other is never any salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for from that all her excellencies have birth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: She hath also for feet and hands the manifestations of her good works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: She hath a soul too, godliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: She hath likewise a bosom of gold, and firmer than adamant, even fortitude; and all may be taken captive more easily than that bosom may be riven asunder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the spirit that is in the brain and heart, is charity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou that in her actual deeds also I show thee her image?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider, I pray thee, this very evangelist: although we have not his whole life in writing, nevertheless even from a few facts one may see his image shine forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First, as to his having been lowly and contrite, hear him, after his gospel, calling himself a publican; for his being also merciful, see him stripping himself of all and following Jesus; and as to his piety, it is evident from his doctrines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And his wisdom again it is easy to see from the gospel which he composed, and his charity(for he cared for the whole world); and the manifestation of his good works, from the throne on which he is to sit;and his courage too, “by his departing with joy from the presence of the council.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us imitate then this virtue, and most of all his humility and almsgiving, without which one cannot be saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is shown by the five virgins, and together with them by the Pharisee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For without virginity indeed it is possible to see the kingdom, but without almsgiving it cannot be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is among the things that are essential, and hold all together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not unnaturally then have we called it the heart of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this heart, unless it supply breath to all, is soon extinguished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the same way then as the fountain also, if it confine its streams to itself, grows putrid; so it is with the rich also, when they keep their possessions to themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore even in our common conversation we say, “great is the consumptionof wealth with such a man;” instead of saying, “great is the abundance, great the treasure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth there is a consumption, not of the possessors only, but of the riches themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since both garments laid by spoil, and gold is cankered, and corn is eaten up, and the soul too of their owner is more than they all cankered and corrupted by the cares of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou be willing to produce in the midst a miser’s soul; like a garment eaten by innumerable worms, and not having any sound part, even so wilt thou find it, perforated on all sides by cares; rotted, cankered by sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not such the poor man’s soul, the soul of him, I mean, that is voluntarily poor; but it is resplendent as gold, it shines like a pearl, and it blooms like a rose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no moth is there, no thief is there, no worldly care, but as angels converse, so do they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wouldest thou see the beauty of this soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wouldest thou acquaint thyself with the riches of poverty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He commands not men, but he commands evil spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He stands not at a king’s side, but he hath taken his stand near to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He is the comrade, not of men, but of angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He hath not chests, two, or three, or twenty, but such an abundance as to account the whole world as nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He hath not a treasure, but heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He needs not slaves, or rather hath his passions for slaves, hath for slaves the motivesthat rule over kings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that which commands him who wears the purple, that motive shrinks before him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And royalty, and gold, and all such things, he laughs at, as at children’s toys; and like hoops, and dice, and heads, and balls, so doth he count all these to be contemptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he hath an adorning, which they who play with these things cannot even see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then can be superior to this poor man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He hath at least heaven for his pavement; but if the pavement be like this, imagine the roof!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But hath he not horses and chariots?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, what need hath he of these, who is to be borne upon the clouds, and to be with Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having these things then impressed on our minds, let us, both men and women, seek after that wealth, and the plenty that cannot be rifled; that we may attain also unto the kingdom of heaven, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
